MINI-MINUTES FOR OCT-07-2011 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT DID NOT BREACH ITS DUTY OF CARE BECAUSE DEFENDANT DID NOT HAVE SUFFICIENT NOTICE OF THE DANGER AND THE ASSAULT HAPPENED SO QUICKLY THAT DEFENDANT COULD NOT HAVE PREVENTED IT. (SEE LOPEZ V. SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA. RTD (1985) 40 CAL.3D 780, 791; CCSF V. SUPERIOR COURT (1994) 31 CAL.APP.4TH 45). PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G). JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-07-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. TERENCE A. REDMOND, TEL. NO. 395-9000 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. WARREN METLITZKY, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3916 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS : DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant did not breach its duty of care because defendant did not have sufficient notice of the danger and the assault happened so quickly that defendant could not have prevented it. (See Lopez v. Southern California. RTD (1985) 40 Cal.3d 780, 791; CCSF v. Superior Court (1994) 31 Cal.App.4th 45). Prevailing party to prepare a form of order pursuant to CCP 437 C(g). Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077 = (302/HEK)
